Entity_Description: Cordova Bay is a seaside neighbourhood of Saanich, British Columbia, on the eastern shore of the Saanich Peninsula about 10 km north of downtown Victoria. Home to roughly 8,000 residents (2021 Census), it is known for its long sandy beach fronting Haro Strait, the Mattick's Farm shopping area beside Cordova Bay Golf Course, and the Lochside Regional Trail along its western edge. Housing ranges from post-war bungalows to waterfront homes, all within Canada's mildest sub-Mediterranean climate in the Olympic Mountains rain shadow.

Short_Description: Cordova Bay is where Greater Victoria goes to the beach — a long crescent of sand on Haro Strait that gives this Saanich neighbourhood its name and its character. From waterfront homes strung along Cordova Bay Road to quiet streets near Mattick's Farm and the Cordova Bay Golf Course, roughly 8,000 residents enjoy island-pace living just 10 km from downtown Victoria. That same setting, though, means salt spray, sea winds, and a mix of older bungalows and high-value waterfront properties that deserve careful hands. {COMPANY_NAME} delivers {SERVICE} built for exactly these conditions, with a local {BUSINESS_TYPE} team that knows the neighbourhood street by street. {CTA_PRIMARY} — call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Cordova Bay is the rare Greater Victoria neighbourhood where the beach is the main street. The long sandy shoreline on Haro Strait draws walkers year-round, and the homes that face it — from renovated post-war bungalows to substantial waterfront properties — take the full brunt of salt air and onshore weather. Anyone providing {SERVICE} here learns quickly that a house east of Cordova Bay Road ages differently than one tucked inland near the Lochside Regional Trail, and {COMPANY_NAME} plans every job around that difference.

The neighbourhood sits on the eastern shore of the Saanich Peninsula, about 10 km north of downtown Victoria, with Highway 17 (the Pat Bay Highway) running along its western edge. That geography shapes daily life: many residents commute south to provincial government offices in Victoria or to the University of Victoria, while others are retirees who chose Cordova Bay precisely for its mild, sub-Mediterranean climate — dry summers, wet winters, and far less rain than Vancouver thanks to the Olympic Mountains rain shadow. Those wet winters concentrate moisture problems into a few months, and the dry summers are when smart owners book {SERVICE} before the rains return.

Local landmarks anchor the community. Mattick's Farm, with its shops and the adjacent Cordova Bay Golf Course, is the neighbourhood's gathering point at the north end. To the south rises Mount Douglas (PKOLS), whose forested slopes mark the boundary with the rest of Saanich. Between them, the housing stock tells the Saanich story in miniature: post-war bungalows from the neighbourhood's first big growth wave, infill family homes, and premium beachfront properties where {PROPERTY_TYPE} owners expect meticulous work.

Coverage is straightforward from here. Central Saanich is barely 1 km away, the broader Saanich core about 4 km, and Gordon Head 5 km down the coast — so when Cordova Bay calls, {COMPANY_NAME} is already in the neighbourhood, not crossing the region to get there. {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP}

Whether it is a salt-weathered home above the beach or a golf-course-adjacent property near Mattick's Farm, {COMPANY_NAME} brings {YEARS_IN_BUSINESS} years of {SERVICE_CATEGORY} experience and a {RATING} reputation to every Cordova Bay job. Reach the team at {PHONE} or {EMAIL} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population: approximately 8,000 (2021 Census)
- Seaside neighbourhood of the District of Saanich, within the Capital Regional District
- About 10 km north of downtown Victoria and 87 km from Vancouver
- Fronts Haro Strait with one of Greater Victoria's best-known sandy beaches
- Home to the Mattick's Farm shopping area and the adjacent Cordova Bay Golf Course
- The Lochside Regional Trail and Highway 17 (Pat Bay Highway) run along its western edge
- Sits in the Olympic Mountains rain shadow — dry summers, wet winters, minimal snow
